explanation: This LTL specification asserts that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will transition to the TRANSITION state in the next time step if the pilot is not in control (meaning the standby atomic proposition is false). The N_DURATION parameter is not strictly necessary for this specific property and is set to 1 for completeness.  Template 11 is chosen because it directly expresses the next-state behavior based on a precondition.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, and the pilot is not in control (i.e., !standby is true), then in the next state, the autopilot will be in TRANSITION.  The N_DURATION is set to 1, as we only care about the next state transition.

This LTL property checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY, it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is not in control. bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY, and bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot transitions to TRANSITION.  The implication (bool_exp2 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3)) ensures that if the autopilot is in STANDBY, then within N_DURATION steps, the TRANSITION state will be reached if pilot is not in control (i.e. !standby). The G operator ensures this holds globally.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will transition to the TRANSITION state in the next time step if the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby).  The N_DURATION parameter is set to 1 because we are interested in the immediate next time step transition.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units, given that the pilot is not in control (standby is false).

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, it will transition to TRANSITION mode in the next time step if the pilot is not in control.  bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY mode (state_is_STANDBY). bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot transitions to TRANSITION mode in the next time step (X state_is_TRANSITION). The implication (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)) ensures that the transition happens only when the autopilot is in STANDBY. The G operator ensures that this implication always holds.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is not in control.  bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY), and bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot is in TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION) and the pilot is not in control (!standby). The formula uses template 14 to express this behavior.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is not in control.  bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY, and bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot is in TRANSITION state and the pilot is not in control. The formula uses template ID 14, which checks for the eventuality of bool_exp3 given bool_exp2.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will transition to the TRANSITION state in the next time step if the pilot is not in control.  The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, achieved by using the atomic proposition state_is_STANDBY. The boolean expression bool_exp3 represents the condition that the pilot is not in control, achieved by negating the atomic proposition standby.  The chosen template ensures the transition occurs in the next time step.  N_DURATION is set to 1 to emphasize this immediate transition.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, and the pilot is not in control (i.e., !standby is true), then in the next time step, the autopilot will transition to the TRANSITION state.  The N_DURATION parameter is set to 1 to reflect this immediate transition requirement.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, it will transition to TRANSITION mode in the next time step if the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby mode).  The N_DURATION parameter is set to 1, which is reasonable given the immediate state transition described in the natural language. We use the atomic proposition "!standby" to indicate that the pilot is not in control.
This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY, it transitions to TRANSITION in the next time step if the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby).

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY, it transitions to TRANSITION in the next state if the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby).  The N_DURATION parameter is set to 1 to enforce this transition to happen immediately in the next step.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY, and the pilot is not in control, then in the next state the autopilot will transition to TRANSITION.  bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY mode and the pilot is not in control (standby is false).  bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot transitions to the TRANSITION state. The G operator ensures this property holds globally across all states.

This LTL specification states that globally, whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, the next state will be TRANSITION if the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby mode).  The use of X (next) operator captures the change of state. The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the current state being STANDBY and the boolean expression bool_exp3 represents the next state being TRANSITION and the pilot not being in control. The N_DURATION parameter is set to 1.

This LTL property states that whenever the system is in STANDBY mode, it will transition to TRANSITION mode in the next time step if the pilot is not in control.  The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition of being in STANDBY mode (state_is_STANDBY), and bool_exp3 represents the condition of the pilot not being in control (!standby). The chosen template ensures that the transition occurs immediately after the STANDBY condition is met, and that the transition is only triggered when the pilot is not in control.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is not in control. bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY). bool_exp3 represents the condition that the pilot is not in control (!standby). The formula ensures that if the autopilot is in STANDBY and the pilot is not in control, then eventually within N_DURATION time units the autopilot will transition to the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION).

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will transition to the TRANSITION state in the next time step if the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby).  It uses a next state operator to capture the transition from one state to the other. The boolean expression bool_exp2 checks for the current state being STANDBY, and bool_exp3 checks for the next state being TRANSITION and the pilot not being in control. The G operator ensures this property holds globally, and the -> operator represents implication or the "if-then" relationship.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, it will transition to TRANSITION mode in the next time step if the pilot is not in control. This is achieved by using the implication operator. The antecedent is state_is_STANDBY (autopilot is in standby mode). The consequent is X state_is_TRANSITION (in the next state, the autopilot is in transition mode) and  !standby (the pilot is not in control).  The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally across all time steps.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is not in control. bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY, and bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot is in TRANSITION and the pilot is not in control. The formula uses template 14 to express this requirement.

This LTL property states that, globally, whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, then in the next state it will be in TRANSITION state if the pilot is not in control.  The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY). The boolean expression bool_exp3 represents the condition that the pilot is not in control (!standby). The next operator X ensures that the transition occurs in the next step. The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, and the pilot is not in control (indicated by !standby),  the autopilot will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units.  The G operator ensures this condition holds globally across all time steps. The -> operator implies the transition. The F operator specifies that eventually the condition will be met. The [0, N_DURATION] interval bounds the time it takes for the transition to occur.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will transition to the TRANSITION state in the next time step if the pilot is not in control.  The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition where the autopilot is in STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY). The boolean expression bool_exp3 represents the condition where the pilot is not in control (!standby). The template ensures that the transition happens in the next time step. N_DURATION is set to 1 as there is no duration associated with this requirement.

The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY, and bool_exp3 represents the condition that the pilot is not in control. The template ensures that the transition happens immediately after the condition is met.

This LTL property checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY, it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is not in control. bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY (state_is_STANDBY). bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot is in TRANSITION (state_is_TRANSITION). The implication (bool_exp2 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3)) ensures that if the autopilot is in STANDBY, it eventually transitions to TRANSITION within the specified time bound. The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally at all times.

The implication (bool_exp2 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3)) ensures that if the autopilot is in STANDBY, then within N_DURATION time units, the TRANSITION state will be reached provided the pilot is not in control. The G operator globally ensures that this holds at all times.

The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition where the autopilot is in STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY). The expression bool_exp3 represents the condition that the pilot is not in control (!standby). The chosen template ensures that this transition happens in the next time instant.

The formula uses the next operator X to ensure the transition happens immediately after the condition is met.  N_DURATION is set to 1 to reflect the immediate nature of the state change.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby state).  The formula uses the implication (->) to express the conditional relationship between the autopilot being in STANDBY and eventually transitioning to TRANSITION, and the boolean expression (!standby) to capture the condition that the pilot is not in control. The G operator ensures that this property holds globally (at all time steps).

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY, it transitions to TRANSITION in the next state if the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby).  The N_DURATION parameter is set to 1 to reflect the immediate transition requirement.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is not in control. bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY, and bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot is in TRANSITION state and the pilot is not in control. The formula uses template ID 14 to express this behavior.

This LTL formula states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY, it will transition to TRANSITION in the next time step if the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby).  The formula uses the atomic propositions state_is_STANDBY and !standby to represent the autopilot's state and pilot control status respectively.  It uses the X operator to indicate the next state transition.  N_DURATION is set to 1, as we don't want to impose any timing constraints beyond the next step.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, then in the next time step it will transition to TRANSITION state if the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby).  The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition of the autopilot being in STANDBY, and bool_exp3 represents the condition of the autopilot transitioning to the TRANSITION state in the next time step when the pilot is not in control. The property uses the template with index 11: G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)).

This LTL formula captures the requirement that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will transition to the TRANSITION state in the next time step if the pilot is not in control.  bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY, and bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot transitions to TRANSITION in the next time step. The implication ensures that the transition happens only when the autopilot is in STANDBY, and the G operator ensures that this implication holds globally across all time steps.

This captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from STANDBY to TRANSITION when the pilot is not in control.

This LTL property states that whenever the system is in STANDBY mode, it will transition to TRANSITION mode in the next time step if the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby mode).  The use of next operator (X) captures the requirement of changing state in the *next* time step. The N_DURATION parameter is set to 1 because this describes an immediate transition and does not cover a duration of time.

This LTL property specifies that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will transition to the TRANSITION state in the next time step if the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby).  The chosen template, 11, directly expresses this next-state relationship using the implication operator.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is not in control (standby is false).  The G operator ensures this holds globally, meaning at all times. The implication (->) connects the precondition (autopilot in STANDBY) to the consequent (eventual transition to TRANSITION). The F operator indicates the eventual occurrence of the TRANSITION state.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby). The atomic proposition "!standby" represents the condition where the pilot is not in control. The formula uses the "G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3))" template where bool_exp2 is the STANDBY condition and bool_exp3 is the condition of changing to the TRANSITION state.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY, it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is not in control (standby is false).  G[0,N_DURATION] ensures the transition happens within the specified duration, | G(!bool_exp3) accounts for the case where the condition is always false, and the implication is used to define the condition for the transition.

This LTL specification uses template 20 to capture the behavior. bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY). bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot transitions to TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION). The formula ensures that whenever the autopilot leaves the STANDBY state (!state_is_STANDBY & X state_is_STANDBY), then in the next state, the autopilot will be in the TRANSITION state (X state_is_TRANSITION). Additionally, it verifies that if the autopilot is currently in the STANDBY state, then it should be in the TRANSITION state in the next state.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, it will transition to TRANSITION mode in the next time step if the pilot is not in control.  This is expressed using implication (->). bool_exp2 represents the condition of being in STANDBY mode, and bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot transitions to TRANSITION in the next state. The negation of "standby" indicates the pilot is not in control.  The G operator ensures this behavior holds globally across all time steps.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY state, it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is not in control. bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY). bool_exp3 represents the condition that the pilot is not in control (!standby) and the autopilot is in TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION). The formula ensures that if the autopilot is in STANDBY, then it eventually transitions to TRANSITION within the specified duration, under the condition that the pilot is not in control.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, it will transition to TRANSITION mode in the next time step if the pilot is not in control (standby is false).  It uses template 11 which expresses a transition of the form bool_exp2 -> X bool_exp3.  bool_exp2 is defined as state_is_STANDBY, and bool_exp3 as state_is_TRANSITION & !standby.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, it will transition to TRANSITION mode in the next time step if the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby mode).  The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the current state being STANDBY, and bool_exp3 represents the next state being TRANSITION and the pilot not being in control. The template ensures that this transition happens immediately after STANDBY.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, and the pilot is not in control (standby is false), then in the next state, the autopilot will be in TRANSITION mode.  The N_DURATION parameter is set to 1 to reflect the immediate transition requirement.

The boolean expressions are straightforward mappings of the natural language descriptions to the atomic propositions.  N_DURATION is set to 1 to represent a single time step.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY, it transitions to TRANSITION in the next state if the pilot is not in control.  bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY (state_is_STANDBY). bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot transitions to TRANSITION in the next state (X state_is_TRANSITION). The overall formula ensures that this transition happens whenever the pilot is not in control (!standby).
